locus_id: SLC24A5 (chr15 LD block 1); representative rs1426654 — cluster of 4 credible SNPs (rs1426654, rs2413887, rs1834640, rs2675345)
nearest_gene_label: SLC24A5 (region/passenger genes CTXN2, LINC01491 tagged by rs2413887/rs1834640/rs2675345)
population: Ethiopia, Tanzania, Botswana; ethnically diverse Africans (2
evidence_quote: Beta(SE)=7.69(0.44); F-test p=5.5×10−62 (GWAS LMM); CAVIAR candidate causal; per-locus variance explained SLC24A5=12.8% (SE 3.5%)
author_explanation_quote: Genetic evidence indicates that the light pigmentation variant at SLC24A5 was introduced into East Africa by gene flow from non-Africans

locus_id: MFSD12 (chr19); representative rs10424065 — cluster of 6 candidate/credible SNPs (rs10424065, rs56203814, rs10414812, rs112332856, rs6510760, rs73527942)
nearest_gene_label: MFSD12 (upstream regulatory SNPs rs112332856/rs6510760 intergenic MFSD12/HMG20B)
population: Ethiopia, Tanzania, Botswana; ethnically diverse Africans (2
evidence_quote: MFSD12 encodes a lysosomal protein that affects melanogenesis in zebrafish and mice
classification_notes: MFSD12 not canonical but Crawford2017 provides direct CRISPR/zebrafish functional evidence: 'MFSD12 encodes a lysosomal protein that affects melanogenesis in zebrafish and mice' -- validated via CRISPR knockout in zebrafish. Strongest single piece of functional-screen evidence for a non-canonical effector in this corpus; corroborates Ang2023 idx39.
author_explanation_quote: MFSD12 encodes a lysosomal protein that affects melanogenesis in zebrafish and mice...MFSD12-HA co-localized with LAMP2...localizes to late endosomes and/or lysosomes
source_location: Crawford2017_Science_AfricanPigmentation.pdf p.5-7 (lysosomal transporter protein section; CRISPR zebrafish)

locus_id: DDB1/TMEM138 region (~195 kb, chr11); representative rs7948623 — 33 CAVIAR candidate SNPs (extract lists 6: rs7948623, rs1377457, rs148172827, rs11230664, rs7120594, rs12289370)
nearest_gene_label: TMEM138 (region/passenger genes DDB1, TMEM138, TMEM216/TMEM025, TKFC; function ascribed to DDB1 UV-response)
population: Ethiopia, Tanzania, Botswana; ethnically diverse Africans (2
evidence_quote: mutations in melanocyte-specific regulatory regions near DDB1/TMEM138 correlate with expression of UV response genes under selection in Eurasians
classification_notes: Paper explicitly names DDB1 (not TMEM138, the nearest-gene label) as the functional target: 'mutations in melanocyte-specific regulatory regions near DDB1/TMEM138 correlate with expression of UV response genes under selection in Eurasians.' DDB1 not canonical; mygene.info confirms xeroderma pigmentosum (XPE) / nucleotide-excision-repair link -- DNA-repair role, not core melanogenesis, but directly UV-response/pigmentation-adjacent with a demonstrated regulatory mechanism in this paper's own text. TMEM138 (the nearest positional label) is NOT the functional target per the authors.
author_explanation_quote: mutations in melanocyte-specific regulatory regions near DDB1/TMEM138 correlate with expression of UV response genes under selection in Eurasians
source_location: Crawford2017_Science_AfricanPigmentation.pdf p.3, p.8 (UV response targets of selection section)

locus_id: OCA2/HERC2 region (chr15); representative rs1800404 — 10 CAVIAR SNPs in 3 clusters (extract lists 5 GWAS-significant: rs1800404, rs1868333, rs735066 [OCA2 region 1]; rs4932620, rs1667393 [HERC2])
nearest_gene_label: OCA2 (region includes HERC2; rs4932620/rs1667393 intron HERC2)
population: Ethiopia, Tanzania, Botswana; ethnically diverse Africans (2
evidence_quote: CAVIAR posterior=1.0 (highest region-1); haplotype→exon-10 inclusion/exclusion p=9.1×10−40; each light T allele reduces exon-10 inclusion ~20% (CI 17.9-21.5%)
classification_notes: OCA2 canonical; exon-10 splicing mechanism, CAVIAR posterior=1.0.
author_explanation_quote: we observe a significant association between a haplotype tagged by rs1800404 and alternative splicing resulting in inclusion/exclusion of exon 10...encodes the amino acids encompassing the third transmembrane domain of OCA2

locus_id: HERC2 rs6497271 (chr15; independent LD block ~437 bp from European eye-color SNP rs12913832)
population: Ethiopia, Tanzania, Botswana; ethnically diverse Africans (2
evidence_quote: Beta(SE)=−1.86(0.39); F-test p=1.8×10−6 (does NOT reach genome-wide significance); independent LD block
classification_notes: HERC2 canonical; not significant in this African dataset (P=1.8e-6, not genome-wide significant) but mechanism (SOX2-motif enhancer regulating MITF) is cited from prior European work.
author_explanation_quote: SNP rs6497271...has been associated with skin color in Europeans and is in a consensus SOX2 motif (a transcription factor which modulates levels of MITF in melanocytes)
notes: paper_verdict in discordance_loci.csv is not_significant (does not reach genome-wide significance in this African dataset), but authors DO propose a regulatory mechanism (SOX2-motif enhancer) citing prior European work -- mechanism_proposed status reflects explanatory content, independent of significance in this cohort.

evidence_quote: In contrast to the lack of variation at MC1R, which is under purifying selection in Africa (61)
classification_notes: MC1R canonical; not an association hit in this row -- cited only as a monomorphic/purifying-selection comparator, not a discovered locus.
author_explanation_quote: In contrast to the lack of variation at MC1R, which is under purifying selection in Africa
notes: MC1R is not a tested association locus here but a stated comparator; authors explicitly attribute its monomorphism to purifying selection -- a mechanism for absence of variation, not a locus classification in the GWAS sense.
